zh.lifeSummary: 當Mstislav III Boris Prince Of Kiev於1154年出生在俄羅斯Владимирская ОбластьВладимир時，他的父親Roman I Rostislavich Grand Prince of Kiev, Prince of Smolensk25歲，母親Marie Swiatoslavna  Novgorod- of Seversky18歲。他和Rani Vasikovna至少有5個兒子和2個女兒。他去世於1223年5月31日在俄羅斯Марий ЭлУдельная享年69歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Mstislav III Boris Prince Of Kiev于1154年出生在俄罗斯Владимирская ОбластьВладимир时，他的父亲Roman I Rostislavich Grand Prince of Kiev, Prince of Smolensk25岁，母亲Marie Swiatoslavna  Novgorod- of Seversky18岁。他和Rani Vasikovna至少有5个儿子和2个女儿。他去世于1223年5月31日在俄罗斯Марий ЭлУдельная享年69岁。

nameMeanings.name: Kiev
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Austrian German: occupational name for a cowherd, Chüyger in the Tyrolean dialect, from Kühe ‘cows’ (plural of Kuh) + -er suffix of agent nouns.
Scottish (Ayrshire and Lanarkshire): variant of   Keir  .

variants.name: Kear
meanings: English (Gloucestershire): occupational name for a locksmith, from Middle English keyer, kayer ‘key-maker, locksmith’ (Old English cǣgere, a derivative of cǣg ‘key’).

nameMeanings.name: Mstislav
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.

nameMeanings.name: III
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Japanese Yuko, Aiko, Hideaki, Hiroki, Hiroyuki, Isamu, Kaoru, Katsumi, Keiichiro, Kenji, Masako, Masayuki.
Japanese: variously written, sometimes with characters used phonetically; the most probable meanings are 仁井 ‘benevolent well’, 新居 ‘new residence’, or 新井 ‘new well’. It is not common in Japan.

nameMeanings.name: Boris
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: from the Tartar nickname Bogoris ‘small’. Later, however, it was taken to be a shortened form of the Russian name Borislav, from bor ‘battle’ + slav ‘glory’. The name was borne in the 9th century by a ruler of Bulgaria who converted his kingdom to Christianity and gave shelter to disciples of saints Cyril and Methodius when they were expelled from Moravia. The name was also borne by a 10th-century Russian saint, son of Prince Vladimir of Kiev and brother of St Gleb . It is as a result of his influence that Boris is one of the very few non-classical names that the Orthodox Church allows to be taken as a baptismal name (although the saint himself bore the baptismal name Romanus). It is borne by the British journalist and politician Boris Johnson ( b. 1964 ).

nameMeanings.name: Prince
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Originally a nickname from the royal title, Old French prince (Latin princeps). The name is popular among Blacks in Britain and the United States, influenced perhaps by the fame of the American singer Prince ( b. 1958 as Prince Rogers Nelson ).

nameMeanings.name: Of
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Short form of Oswald or any of the various other names beginning with Os-.

en.lifeSummary: When Mstislav III Boris Prince Of Kiev was born in 1154, in Vladimir, Vladimir Oblast, Russia, his father, Roman I Rostislavich Grand Prince of Kiev, Prince of Smolensk, was 25 and his mother, Marie Swiatoslavna  Novgorod- of Seversky, was 18. He had at least 5 sons and 2 daughters with Rani Vasikovna. He died on 31 May 1223, in udelnaya, Mari El, Russia, at the age of 69.
